title: gh#31: Phase 6 leftovers: worker TS build errors + posts-api test failure on develop
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/31.
Backlog section: Bugs.

Two pre-existing failures from Phase 6 work that are present on `develop` today and were observed during the Phase 7 PR (#30) review cycle. Filing so they don't get lost — neither was caused by Phase 7 and both were intentionally left out of scope for that PR.

## 1. `@sms/worker` TypeScript build fails

`pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit` (or the workspace `pnpm build`) errors out with:

```
src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts(108,10): error TS2339: Property 'emit' does not exist on type 'ChildProcess'.
src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts(119,10): error TS2339: Property 'emit' does not exist on type 'ChildProcess'.
src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts(132,10): error TS2339: Property 'emit' does not exist on type 'ChildProcess'.
src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts(171,10): error TS2339: Property 'emit' does not exist on type 'ChildProcess'.
src/transcode.service.ts(49,10): error TS2339: Property 'on' does not exist on type 'ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams'.
src/transcode.service.ts(49,23): error TS7006: Parameter 'code' implicitly has an 'any' type.
src/transcode.service.ts(64,10): error TS2339: Property 'on' does not exist on type 'ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams'.
src/transcode.service.ts(64,23): error TS7006: Parameter 'err' implicitly has an 'any' type.
```

`ChildProcess` is an `EventEmitter` so `.on()` / `.emit()` should exist via `node:events`. The most likely root cause is a `@types/node` resolution mismatch — `packages/db` and `packages/shared` declare `@types/node` `^25.5.2` directly, but `packages/worker` does not declare it at all and presumably picks up types via hoisting. Worth confirming whether the worker should pin its own `@types/node` so the resolution is deterministic.

**Workarounds in place:** runtime tests pass via `vitest run` (which doesn't go through `tsc`), so worker behavior is verified end-to-end. The build failure only blocks the workspace `pnpm build` script.

**Files involved:**
- `packages/worker/src/transcode.service.ts:49,64`
- `packages/worker/src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts:108,119,132,171`
- `packages/worker/package.json` (missing `@types/node`)

## 2. `posts-api.test.ts` — empty array vs `undefined` for `mediaIds`

```
FAIL  packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts
  > posts API integration > media association
  > POST /api/posts without mediaIds passes undefined mediaIds to createPost
AssertionError: expected [] to be undefined
```

Test at `packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts:565` asserts:

```ts
expect(postInput.mediaIds).toBeUndefined();
```

…but the route is now passing `[]` to `createPost`. Either the route handler started defaulting `mediaIds` to `[]` somewhere along the way, or the test's expected behavior is stale. Need to decide which is the intended contract:

- If `createPost` should receive `undefined` when no media is attached → fix the route handler to omit the field
- If `createPost` should always receive an array (possibly empty) → update the test + the function signature/typing

**Files involved:**
- `packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts:565` (the failing assertion)
- `packages/api/src/routes/posts.ts` (route handler that builds the `createPost` input)

## Verification

Both failures reproduce on `develop` at `95759aa` and on every recent commit before that. Neither was introduced by PR #30.

## Suggested next steps

1. File a follow-up PR for the worker `@types/node` issue — likely a one-line `package.json` change plus a smoke test of `pnpm build`.
2. Triage the `mediaIds` contract decision before fixing — probably a quick conversation about whether routes normalize to `[]` or pass through `undefined`.
recommendation:
1. File a follow-up PR for the worker `@types/node` issue — likely a one-line `package.json` change plus a smoke test of `pnpm build`.
2. Triage the `mediaIds` contract decision before fixing — probably a quick conversation about whether routes normalize to `[]` or pass through `undefined`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
suggestedRegressionTest: Both failures reproduce on `develop` at `95759aa` and on every recent commit before that. Neither was introduced by PR #30.
minimumFixScope: packages/worker/src/index.ts, packages/db, packages/shared, packages/worker, packages/worker/package.json, packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts, packages/api/src/routes/posts.ts, package.json

history.reasoning: Original evidence paths still exist. The specific Phase 6 transcode TypeScript errors appear fixed: packages/worker/src/transcode.service.ts now uses a local FfmpegProcess interface, packages/worker/src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts defines MockChildProcess extends EventEmitter, and rerunning the worker typecheck no longer reports ChildProcess/.on/.emit errors. The posts-api mediaIds assertion also appears fixed/stale: packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts:558-571 now expects [] and packages/shared/src/schemas/posts.ts defines mediaIds with .default([]), while packages/api/src/routes/posts.ts forwards parsed.data to createPost. However the worker TypeScript build is still currently failing elsewhere: pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit reports packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.impl.ts(79,62) TS2345. Because the finding's worker-build failure remains open in current code, although with moved/different evidence, the finding is still open. I could not execute the posts-api Vitest suite in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/Vite attempted to create temp files and failed with EPERM; api tsc also currently fails on an unrelated service type error.
commands: rg --files packages/worker packages/api/src/__tests__/integration packages/api/src/routes packages/db packages/shared | sort

history.reasoning: All original evidence paths still exist. The worker TypeScript failure is fixed in the current repository: packages/worker/src/transcode.service.ts now uses a local FfmpegProcess interface, packages/worker/src/__tests__/transcode.test.ts uses MockChildProcess extends EventEmitter, and `pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0 with no ChildProcess/.on/.emit errors. The posts-api mediaIds failure is also fixed/stale: the old assertion at packages/api/src/__tests__/integration/posts-api.test.ts:565 no longer expects undefined; the test now expects `postInput.mediaIds` to equal `[]`, packages/shared/src/schemas/posts.ts defaults mediaIds to `[]`, and packages/api/src/routes/posts.ts forwards parsed.data to createPost. I could not execute the Vitest suites in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/Vite fails before loading tests with EPERM creating its temp SSR directory; `pnpm --filter @sms/api exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` still fails, but on an unrelated `scheduled_at_invalid` type error outside this finding.
